ground_truth.title: Your Ring doorbell now lets Alexa+ answer the door for you
ground_truth.author: Pranob Mehrotra
ground_truth.main_content: Following the recent launch of Alexa+ on the web, Amazon is now bringing its AI assistant to your Ring doorbell. The company has announced a new feature that lets Alexa answer the door and hold short conversations with visitors, even when you're not at home. The feature, called Alexa+ Greetings, allows Ring doorbells to respond to people at your door using conversational AI. When someone rings the bell, Alexa can detect what's happening using the doorbell's camera and audio feed, and then reply accordingly. For example, it can ask a delivery driver to leave a package in a specific spot, tell a salesperson you're not available, or greet a familiar visitor and take a message. Amazon says the system relies on a combination of Ring's existing video summaries and Alexa's upgraded generative AI capabilities. It lets users set up custom interactions through the Ring app, telling Alexa exactly how to respond in different situations. The conversations are recorded and saved alongside the video clip, letting users review the interaction later. Alexa+ Greetings effectively turns Ring doorbells into an intelligent assistant capable of determining who's at your door, understanding what they need and responding conversationally. Instead of checking alerts or the live view from the doorbell, Alexa can handle basic interactions on your behalf, which can be useful if you're away or busy. The feature shows how Amazon is positioning Alexa+ as a more versatile assistant that extends beyond smart speakers and displays. For Ring users already invested in Amazon's ecosystem, it adds another layer of automation to the front door. Amazon says the feature is currently limited to Alexa+ Early Access users in the US and Canada. It only works on select wired Ring doorbell models, requires a Ring subscription, and only supports English.

When someone rings the bell, Alexa can detect what's happening using the doorbell's camera and audio feed, and then reply accordingly.

The feature is currently limited to Alexa+ Early Access users in the US and Canada and only works on select wired Ring doorbell models.
